objective: Document hldpro-sim version management and upgrade workflow for consumer repos. Add a version management section to docs/agents-adoption-guide.md covering version checking, staleness detection, and re-deployment. A standalone drift detector script (scripts/overlord/check_hldpro_sim_version.py) is deferred to a follow-up issue to avoid the worker-handoff gate blocker; this issue is documentation-only.
scope_boundary: docs/agents-adoption-guide.md — append hldpro-sim version management section
out_of_scope: scripts/overlord/check_hldpro_sim_version.py — deferred to follow-up issue to avoid worker-handoff gate blocker
overlord-sweep integration for drift detection — follows the drift detector script
Any changes to packages/hldpro-sim/ or the deployer script itself
Consumer-side CI automation for version pinning
research_summary: hldpro-sim v0.1.0 was authored in issue #407 and deployed via issue #422. The consumer-pull-state record (docs/hldpro-sim-consumer-pull-state.json) captures pinned_sha=fed5ead670cf6834e5c73bffcaf64e41cc483fce, tag hldpro-sim-v0.1.0, and the deployer script path. Consumer repos write .hldpro/hldpro-sim.json at deploy time. No documentation exists in the agents adoption guide covering how to check version currency or trigger a re-deploy. The drift detector script is blocked by worker-handoff gate (prior issue) and will be a follow-up.
research_artifacts: docs/hldpro-sim-consumer-pull-state.json — pinned_sha, deployer script, consumer record path
docs/agents-adoption-guide.md — existing adoption guide to be extended
Issue #422: hldpro-sim deployer and consumer record
Issue #563: this issue — version management documentation

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Documentation-only: append hldpro-sim version management section to docs/agents-adoption-guide.md; write plan, PDCAR, execution scope, and closeout artifacts. No new Python files.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validator PASS confirmed. Proceed to write execution scope, PDCAR, adoption guide append, closeout, and commit.

material_deviation_rules: If any .py file is created during this work, HALT — defer to follow-up issue.
If the adoption guide append modifies existing content (not just appends), HALT and re-scope.
If the closeout hook exits non-zero, fix the artifact before committing.
If the validator returns FAIL at any point, fix and re-run before proceeding.
